channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Meet John Adesoye, Nigerian man who graduated as nurse in UK at 64
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A Nigerian-born man, John Adesoye, has graduated as a registered nurse in the United Kingdom at the age of 64, proving that it is never too late to pursue a new career or earn a degree. The post Meet John Adesoye, Nigerian man who graduated as nurse in UK at 64 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Badejo-Okusanya promises inclusive leadership after emerging NBA President-elect
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, has pledged to unite members of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) and build a more inclusive professional body following her election as the association's President-elect. The post Badejo-Okusanya promises inclusive leadership after eme

item.title: Why we picked woman as my running mate – LP presidential candidate, Okereke
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Okereke said it was the belief of the party that women should be given leadership opportunities and not suppressed. The post Why we picked woman as my running mate – LP presidential candidate, Okereke appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Can you name every winner?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: With the World Cup final taking place on Sunday, put your knowledge of the previous winners to the test.

item.title: “Terrorists Released Us Before Soldiers Took Us Home” – Rescued Oyo Teacher
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: One of the teachers rescued after spending 56 days in the custody of kidnappers in Oyo State, Zacchaeus Olatunde, has claimed that he and the other captives were released by their abductors before security personnel eventually escorted them home. Olatunde made the claim during a telephone interview

item.title: My Son Changed After 56 Days With Bandits — Father Of 2-Year-Old
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Father of one of the abducted pupils of Baptist Nursery and Primary School, Yawota in Orire Local Government of Oyo State has revealed that his son is still suffering from the effects of the abduction. Armed bandits attacked Baptist Nursery and Primary School, Yawota; L.A. Primary School; and Commun

item.title: Eight-month expedition to study the impact of climate change
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: As Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, an adaptation of Homer's epic poem, arrives in cinemas, a very different odyssey is about to begin. Next month, a team of scientists will travel to the remote Arctic town of Kirkenes in Norway to board the floating laboratory Tara Polar Station for an eight-month

item.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

item.title: Why have India’s Gen Z protesters called for a march to parliament?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: After a prominent activist on hunger strike is forcibly removed, organisers announce a march to parliament.

item.title: Seven things to know about new NBA President, Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has elected Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, a Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), as its next president after she secured the highest number of votes in the association’s election, which officially concluded on Sunday morning. Mrs Badejo-Okusanya, the only female among

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Temi's mother, Nana, who was a guest on the show, also weighed in on the discussion, stating that having eleven children will not be a bad thing for the couple.

item.title: “If I Date Broke Guy Earning N5,000 Monthly, He Must Be Giving Me N3,000 Out Of It” – Toke Makinwa
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Nigerian media personality Toke Makinwa has said she has never been in a relationship where a man asked her for financial support. Speaking on the topic of relationships, Toke said she is often surprised when she hears women complain about men asking them for money. According to her, her personal ex

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Her election marks a significant milestone for the legal profession, following the tenure of Priscilla Kuye, who became the association's first female president in 1991. item.description: A 64-year-old Nigerian, John Adesoye, has qualified as a registered nurse in the United Kingdom, describing the achievement as proof that age should never be a barrier to pursuing one’s dreams. According to a report BBC, Adesoye, who was raised in Nigeria, previously worked as an electronics t

item.title: Nigeria launches new push for Niger Delta agri-development
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “Before oil took centre stage in our economy, it was the soil that paid our bills. We must, therefore, return to agriculture for our economic development,” Vice President Shettima said. item.title: England stun France in goal fest for third place
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: England finish third after dominating in the first half. France managed to claw its way back into the game, but did not manage to find the win for Didier Deschamps' last match as French manager. Mbappé becomes the highest scoring player in world cup history, and Michael Olise matches Lionel Messi's

item.title: Andrew Tate arrested in Miami over UK rape, trafficking charges
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Controversial social media personality Andrew Tate and his brother, Tristan Tate, have been arrested in Miami, Florida, following a request by British authorities seeking their extradition to face fresh charges including rape, human trafficking and assault. item.title: Asari Dokubo urges respect for judgment, as court ends 100-year dispute between Rivers communities
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Daniel Abia Port Harcourt — A Rivers State High Court sitting in Port Harcourt has brought a century-old land ownership dispute between Usokun and Obuama communities in Degema Local Government Area to a close, dismissing a suit filed by Usokun Community over the disputed Obupiri land. Justice Dav

item.title: NBA elects Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya as new president
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senior Advocate of Nigeria, Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, has emerged as the 33rd President of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA), defeating fellow senior lawyers Lateef Akangbe, SAN, and Olumuyiwa Akinboro, SAN, in the association's 2026 national election. item.description: According to Goni, the coordinated response by the military and police overwhelmed the insurgents, forcing them to abandon the operation and flee without succeeding in abducting any student.

item.title: ‘Iron sharpens iron’ – Reno Omokri sends birthday message to Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria’s Ambassador designate to Mexico, Reno Omokri, has sent a birthday message to presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Peter Obi. Omokri described Obi as a politician who has helped strengthen democratic participation in Nigeria. Congratulating Obi on his 65th birthday

item.description: By Dennis Agbo Former Southeast spokesman for President Bola Tinubu, Josef Onoh, has paid a heartfelt tribute to the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) presidential candidate, Mr. Peter Obi, as he marks his 65th birthday. item.description: Two firefighters are receiving medical treatment after collapsing while battling a fire that engulfed the top floor of a three-storey residential building in the Ketu-Ikosi area of Lagos State. The Lagos State Emergency Management Agency, LASEMA)l, disclosed the development in a statement issued on&

item.title: RMD joins fight against gender based violence
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: RMD also insisted that ending violence against women is not a struggle women should fight alone, urging men to take responsibility for changing harmful attitudes and behaviors.

item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on video games and physical media, follow Jay Peters. item.title: Can the ‘Magnificent Seven’ save a stock market that might be doomed without them?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Apple, Amazon, Nvidia and peers are quietly coming back to life, and that could easily jolt this sleepy stock market back into gear.

item.title: The CLAP BACK Post
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Some people are too comfortable throwing comments that they can’t defend.They stir drama, twist narratives, and expect everyone to play along.That's not happening here....Clapback or dont complain!!

item.title: The week in pictures: The end of the World Cup, heatwaves and wildfires, war in Ukraine
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The final stages of the World Cup, with Les Bleus’ elimination and England finishing third; extreme heat and wildfires around the world; and the Tour de France brought to a standstill by the heatwave in France. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

item.title: Europe: Extreme weather fuels climate concerns
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Europe is the world's fastest-warming continent, and this summer has already brought record-breaking temperatures, devastating wildfires, hailstorms and tornadoes. While climate change is not responsible for every extreme weather event, scientists say it is making such events more frequent and more

item.title: Russia launches deadly strikes on Kyiv after attack on Moscow warehouses
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Russia fired two dozen ballistic missiles at Kyiv overnight on Sunday, killing one person and wounding 16, in an ever-escalating air war that is taking an increasing civilian toll on both sides. The latest with France 24 correspondent Emmanuelle Chaze, reporting from Kyiv.

item.title: Badejo-Okusanya makes history as NBA’s second female President in 34 years
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Badejo-Okusanya will serve a two-year tenure from 2026 to 2028, succeeding Afam Osigwe, SAN. Her election marks a significant milestone for the association, making her the first woman to lead the NBA in more than three decades after Mrs. Priscilla Kuye, who served as president between 1991 and 1992.

item.title: Osun 2026: Parties sign peace accord ahead of election
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: With less than a month to the poll and the tempo of election-related violence rising, participants stressed the need for restraint and education of all on the imperatives of peace and security.

item.description: Nigerian TikTok creator Peller has said he is eager to marry his fiancée, Jarvis, describing her as someone who complements him. item.title: Access Holdings sells 7.44% stake in Ghana unit
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Access Holdings Plc has completed the sale of a 7.44% stake in its Ghanaian subsidiary, a move analysts say is closely tied to a Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) rule capping how much local banks can hold in foreign units. The post Access Holdings sells 7.44% stake in Ghana unit appeared first on Naira

item.title: Police Rescue Nine Children, Arrest Two Suspected Human Traffickers In Niger
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: One of the suspects admitted that he was to receive ₦10,000 as commission on each child every month, while ₦20,000 would be paid as the child's monthly wage. The post Police Rescue Nine Children, Arrest Two Suspected Human Traffickers In Niger appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: NBA elects Badejo-Okusanya as president, 12 other national officers (FULL RESULTS)
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The elections for all 13 national offices of the NBA recorded a low turnout; only 26,184 voters participated, representing only 31.86 per cent of the total 82,172 registered voters. The post NBA elects Badejo-Okusanya as president, 12 other national officers (FULL RESULTS) appeared first on Premium

item.title: Police Arrest Four Suspected Terrorists In Imo, Recover Biafra Flags
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Operatives of the Nigeria Police Force’s Intelligence Response Unit (VCRU) have arrested four suspected terrorists during a raid on a hideout in Eziudo, Ezinihite Mbaise Local Government Area of Imo State. During the operation, the police recovered three pump-action guns, 30 live cartridges, B

item.title: Badejo-Okusanya Emerges As 33rd NBA President
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN) Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya has been elected the 33rd President of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA). Badejo-Okusanya secured a total of 12,317 votes, representing 47.18 per cent of the total votes cast to defeat her closest challengers, Lateef Akangbe, SAN, who

item.title: ‘Rock who refuses to bow’ – OK Movement hails Peter Obi at 65
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The OK Movement has felicitated with the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi, on his 65th birthday anniversary. In a post on its verified X handle on Sunday, the movement described Obi as a rock that refuses to bow. It also described the 65th birthday anniversary

item.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes.”

item.title: Media Personality Toke Makinwa Reacts To Trolling Over Saying That She Never Dated A Man She Gave Money
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Media personality Toke Makinwa in a recent episode of her podcast Toke Moments asserted she would rather "ghost" a partner than financially maintain him...She said "If I date a broke guy who earns ₦5,000, he must give me ₦3,000 from it".Nigerian Netizens have been trolling her since the comment and

item.title: ‘Tinubu Is The Pillar Of My Infrastructural Development’
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has been a pillar of support in the execution of all road projects and infrastructural development in Kaduna State in the last three and half years. Governor Uba Sani gave the testimony at the flagging off of 11 road projects in Zaria and Sabon Gari local governments at the Fil

item.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Four people are killed and at least 36 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

item.title: 2027: Why I Picked Kwankwaso As My Running Mate – Obi
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) in the forthcoming 2027 general elections, Peter Obi, has opened up on his decision to pick the 2023 presidential candidate of the New Nigeria People’s Party (NNPP) and leader of Kwankwassiyya Movement, Rabiu Kwankwaso, as his

item.title: Badejo-Okusanya makes history, emerges as 2nd female NBA president after 34 years
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: Mrs. Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, has emerged as president after completion of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) 2026 national officers’ election. Chairman of the Electoral Committee of the NBA, Chief Aham Ejelam, SAN, on Sunday announced her as the winner of the presidential seat, despite int

item.title: Uba Sani: Tinubu Is The Pillar Of My Infrastructural Development
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has been a pillar of support in the execution of all road projects and infrastructural development in Kaduna State in the last three and half years. Governor Uba Sani gave the testimony at the flagging off of 11 road projects in Zaria and Sabon Gari local governments at the Fil

item.title: Argentina Bid To Emulate Brazil, Italy Rare World Cup Feat Ahead Spain Clash
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: It will be the battle of the champions as Argentina, led by inspirational captain Lionel Messi, will face Spain in a FIFA World Cup 2026 final. This is no ordinary final, as Argentina stand on the brink of immortality. A victory would make them only the third team in history—and the first since the

item.title: I’ll run inclusive, reform-driven govt if elected in 2027 – Labour Party presidential candidate
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “The message is simple. This is a Nigerian first project; Believe again. Our plan is to change the mindset of young people whose key plan is how to travel abroad. We will make them believe again in the Nigerian project."
